As you know, we've never had a year where that's roughly been less than $60 million going back to 2020, so it's certainly unique to have zero. We don't feel there's anything else out there.

This essentially confirms that the element of revenues that excited the market, and allowed Maravai stock to surge during the pandemic years, will not return, and that the business must focus its energies elsewhere. In that sense, a current market cap valuation of $503m (at the time of writing) for a ~$200m revenue company that expects to be loss-making in 2025 is fair-to-high, I'd speculate. Is there anything management can do to change the narrative going forward?

Looking Ahead - Plenty Of Capacity, Fewer Customers

In essence, Maravai can only be successful if its clients have a high demand for the products it can produce - Maravai says in its 10K:

mRNA lies at the core of our capabilities and expertise. We have developed significant proficiency in mRNA technology, driven by our belief in its transformative potential as a therapeutic modality. The first clinical trial involving an mRNA therapeutic agent took place in 2016. Since then, over 1,500 clinical trials are now in the pipeline, encompassing a wide range of medical applications.

Unfortunately, although the number of ongoing studies figure seems large, mRNA medicines have not progressed as had been hoped during the pandemic, when it was generally believed that post-COVID, this approach could be applied successfully to a host of new indications.

If we think about mRNA specialists, Moderna, Inc. (MRNA) has successfully commercialized a single product since the pandemic, an RSV vaccine whose future earning potential appears to be restricted by competition - Pfizer Inc. (PFE) and AstraZeneca also has RXV vaccines on the market - and a vaccine sceptic new healthcare administration in the U.S.

BioNTech has released no new mRNA products, and neither has Pfizer. Although Maravai says that "mRNA-based programs are addressing various medical conditions," including in oncology, liver disease, cardiovascular disease, and autoimmune conditions, other vaccines, and even cell and gene therapy programs, the lack of progress to date, I would argue, is a long-term concern for Maravai.

Not only that, but Maravai's operates within a competitive marketplace. The company name checks the likes of Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO), Aldeveron, New England Biolabs, Lonza, Catalent - now part of Novo Nordisk A/S (NVO), Qiagen N.V. (QGEN), Roche Holding AG (OTCQX:RHHBY), and Samsung Biologics as competitors. Most if not all of these companies are better resourced than Maravai, and if one product falls out of use, are in a better position to develop new products or pivot towards other products in their portfolio.

On the plus side, Maravai says, "We occupy facilities in San Diego, California, Leland, North Carolina, Sterling, Virginia, and Jupiter, Florida," and these facilities are capable of manufacturing active pharmaceutical ingredients and GMP-grade manufacturing to support biotech/pharma customers. My understanding is that Maravai is leasing these facilities, as opposed to owning them outright.

In the context of the Trump administration's tariffs on imported goods, the fact that all of Maravai's facilities are in the US and therefore tariff-free may attract customers who were formerly using overseas facilities.

In short then, it appears that Maravai's task in 2025 is to go out and attract more customers by offering more innovative and cost-effective solutions than its rivals.

On the earnings call, management notes that its acquisitions of Officinae Bio and Molecular Assemblies last year "bring us unique assets and capabilities to create a new ecosystem in the life science tool space," that its TriLink GMP products and services which utilise its Flanders 1 and 2 manufacturing facilities in San Diego "are primed for future growth in the genomic medicines market," and that its "Alphazyme enzyme business is adding new customers at an exciting rate."

Analysis - Am I Switching My "Sell" Call On Maravai Stock?

I think this is a tight call between "sell," and "hold." Personally, I think the companies face too many headwinds for a "Buy" call to be justified at this time.

Net cash position of just $22m, no more COVID vaccine-related revenues (despite the fact COVID vaccines will likely generate billions in 2025 - either vaccine makers are no longer using Clean Cap, or they have stockpiled sufficient quantities not to need to accumulate more), spare capacity at its manufacturing facilities that is a drain on financial resources, a depressed biotech market (particularly within mRNA and cell therapy, key clients for Maravai), meaning Maravai's customers have less and less money to spend, and a lack of tangible progress within the mRNA field of medicines. The company also has 570 staff, almost a third of whom hold advanced degrees, meaning they command high wages, and it competes against better-resourced and larger rivals.

These are tough challenges, and management admits it has been experiencing challenges in forecasting demand, and that it may need to implement further cost-saving initiatives in order to avoid running into financial difficulties.

Balanced against that are some positives, however. The company says it has "executed 11 new agreements in 2024, bringing our total to 43 license holders," and also estimates "clean cap market share at approximately 30% for clinical stage programs we track, and closer to 40% at the discovery stage, which should drive increased future participation in clinical programs."

My conclusion therefore is to award Maravai a "Hold" rating, upgrading my prior "Sell" call. In summary, it is clear that Maravai's business has suffered badly in recent years, as the business that made the company so initially successful barely exists anymore.

The Maravai LifeSciences Holdings, Inc. share price descent has been ugly, and I am not sure if we have hit the bottom yet. An exit to a rival via M&A is possible, but would any company spend $500m acquiring Maravai's business, which is now more about what may happen going forward than any intrinsic value in the business today?

With that said, I do believe that the biotech industry is currently in a trough that it can recover from, and a rising tide may lift all boats, including Maravai, as budgets increase and companies are prepared to take more budgetary risks.

I don't see that happening in 2025, but neither do I see this being the end of the road for Maravai. If I were a shareholder, I'd cross my fingers for better trading conditions, and keep holding stock.

data.title: Stock Picks From Seeking Alpha's March 2025 New Analysts
data.description:
Showcase Intro

In March, we welcomed 30 new analysts who published their first-ever article on Seeking Alpha. In this article, our editors highlight some of the best ideas from these new analysts and introduce them.

The first five introductions are spotlight features, including a longer excerpt from the analyst's article. The rest of the new analysts will be introduced after these with a more brief excerpt, and organized by rating from Strong Sell to Strong Buy. Each section includes details about the new analysts' interests and backgrounds, so you can get to know them a bit more.

With all the ideas and information shared in this article, I'd invite you to join the conversation and let others know what you think: are any of these picks worth following up on?

To our new analysts: welcome to the community! And please don't hesitate to share more in the comments to introduce yourself to our readers.

Top Stock Picks From Seeking Alpha's New Analysts

Julia Ostian | C3.ai Is Growing, But So Are Its Problems

Biography: “I’m passionate about finance and investing, focusing on business analysis, fundamental analysis, valuation, and long-term growth, especially in sectors like AI, fintech, finance and tech. I study finance and economy and have hands-on experience in equity research, financial modeling, and creating investment content. I actively analyze publicly traded companies with a focus on business models, earnings performance, and competitive positioning. I joined Seeking Alpha to contribute thoughtful, research-backed analysis that helps other investors make better decisions. My goal is to offer clear, unbiased insights into companies' strengths, risks, and valuation to help readers to build their unique opinion and investment strategy.”

Hold | “C3.ai (AI) is in a difficult position. On one hand, it has strong momentum from the growing AI market, solid partnerships with major enterprises, and enough cash to keep running for years without needing more funding. But at the same time, its revenue growth is only okay, not enough to justify high valuation multiple, profitability still feels too far away to even mention, and constant share dilution does not help to fall in love with this stock. The company trades at a premium valuation (P/S ~7.5x), yet it isn’t delivering the type of hyper-growth that justifies such a high multiple. Meanwhile, competition from Palantir, Microsoft, and Google threatens its long-term market positioning.

At this stage, C3.ai is not a buy for me, but it’s also not a sell. It remains a highly speculative AI stock with potential upside—but also significant downside risk if it fails to execute. Personally, I want to sit this one out. I will revisit my decision if macroeconomic conditions improve and if C3.ai’s financial performance shows real signs of acceleration. Until then, patience remains the best strategy for me.”

-

Cyn Research | Robinhood: Long-Term Potential, Short-Term Pain

Biography: “I'm a passionate investor who has been researching publicly traded companies for over 6 years. My primary focus is on identifying great businesses at reasonable prices and holding them for the long term. While I have a slight bias toward technology companies, I maintain a broad perspective, including opportunities in crypto. I take a global approach to investing, occasionally seeking value beyond the U.S. market.”

Hold | “Robinhood (HOOD) remains a fundamentally solid company with an impressive track record and outlook for growth and innovation. However, I believe a drop in revenue growth due to the aforementioned headwinds, such as decreasing interest rates and trading volumes, is currently not being priced in and could lead to significant multiple contraction. Therefore, I view the best course of action for early investors is to just hold.

There are also some aspects to consider in which I might be wrong that could not make the stock drop significantly at all. However, I believe that, with a beta of 3 and 2025 market volatility continuing, there should be an opportunity to buy Robinhood at a more favorable valuation and price in the short- to mid-term.”

-

Eliana Scialabba | Central Puerto: Energy In Evolution, Looking To The Future

Biography: “I am an individual investor with over five years of experience in personal investing, holding a PhD in Economics from UCEMA. I serve as Chief Economist at CEEAXXI and Director of Financial Development at Luxury Entertainment CrowdTech. My investment approach focuses on 'value' companies with solid long-term potential. I share my knowledge with the community by offering analysis to support individual investors.”

Buy | “Central Puerto S.A. (NYSE:CEPU) is an undisputed leader in the Argentine energy market. Additionally, the recent changes both at a macroeconomic level and also at a strategic level for the firm, in my opinion, position it as an actor of growing importance in the medium and long term, not only in the sector, but in the development of the entire transformation that Argentina seeks, and needs, to carry out.

Unlike most companies operating in the country, affected by the volatility inherent in the Argentine macroeconomy, CEPU's contracts with the government, denominated in dollars and long-term, are fundamental for the stability of the company's performance in the medium and long term.

Additionally, the good financial performance and low debt exposure allows CEPU to finance investments in industry and other sectors, as well as continue with its dividend payment policy, which, together with its solid fundamentals, I consider shows its growth potential.”

-

John Duncan | CF Industries: The Cash King Of The Fertilizer Industry

Biography: “John Duncan is an individual investor with over 25 years of experience, primarily focused on value investing using Phil Town’s Rule #1 methodology. While his primary focus is value investing, he occasionally places long-term bets on growth companies that he believes have developing competitive moats. To manage risk, John employs options strategies such as selling out-of-the-money puts on stocks he wants to own and using covered calls to reduce his cost basis in the stock. He analyzes opportunities across various industries, provided he can develop a strong understanding of the business.”

Buy | “CF Industries (CF), the world's largest nitrogen fertilizer producer, boasts an economic moat with low-cost operations and superior logistics, ensuring consistent shareholder value. The company's strategic focus on nitrogen-based products and disciplined capital reinvestment have led to substantial free cash flow and shareholder returns. Future growth is bolstered by ventures into blue and green ammonia, positioning CF ahead of regulatory trends and opening new market opportunities.

CF is a good investment because they lead the competition in most operational metrics, their long-standing low-cost moat is still intact, and they are trading as a large margin of safety to their cash flows and have a 10% yield on cash. CF also has a long track record of consistently returning capital to shareholders.”

-

Kirill Derevytskyy | Kyndryl's Transformation Is Real - And The Market Is Sleeping On It

Biography: “I am an aspiring equity research analyst with a passion for understanding businesses, uncovering mispriced opportunities, and generating alpha. My investing philosophy follows a growth at a reasonable price (GARP) approach, blending fundamental research with valuation discipline to find companies with strong competitive advantages, margin expansion potential, and long-term efficiency gains. Currently, I am studying Economics, Finance, and Data Science at Imperial College London, where I apply quantitative and qualitative frameworks to investment analysis. I have gained hands-on experience through equity research internships and portfolio management, refining my ability to analyze financial statements, model future earnings, and assess risk-reward profiles. Beyond academia, I co-founded Capital Compass, where I publish research on macro trends, valuation techniques, and investment strategies.”

Buy | “Kyndryl Holdings (NYSE: KD) is an underappreciated IT infrastructure and consulting services company that is going through a strategic transformation with massive margin expansion, free cash flow growth, and revaluation potentials. Starting from its IBM separation in 2021, Kyndryl has aggressively terminated low-margin contracts and rebranded as a digital integrator, benefiting from hybrid cloud, cybersecurity, AI, and IT consulting. However, despite significant improvements, the market continues to price KD as a “melting ice cube,” underestimating the potential for a revival and immense growth opportunities.

With the risk of a slowdown in the short run, already majorly reflected in a 15% price decline, I believe KD presents a unique asymmetric risk/reward investment with a cheap price for a company with rapid business momentum that dominates its industry. Yet, when we factor in the sensitivity of valuation to any deviation in growth and Kyndryl being at an early stage, I believe KD stock is not a Strong Buy yet, but with a target price of $49 and 39% potential upside, it deserves a Buy rating.”

New Analyst Stock Ideas From March 2025

Joseph Noko | Skyline Builders: Sky High Pricing For Low-Growth, Low Profitability

Biography: “My investment approach is rooted in fundamental analysis, forensic accounting, and a structured valuation framework. I make forensic accounting adjustments to financial statements in order to more accurately calculate key metrics such as NOPAT and ROIC. Using reverse DCF modelling, I extract market expectations and assess performance scenarios, ensuring valuations are grounded in fundamentals rather than speculation.”

Strong Sell | “Skyline Builders Group Holding Limited (SKBL) is rated Unattractive due to regulatory risks, competitive pressures, and financial instability, despite its strong growth potential and market position.”

-

The Autistic Investor | Moncler Jackets Worth The Price, The Stock Not Yet

Biography: “I focus on sectors that are both exciting and easy to understand. My approach is rooted in fundamental analysis: I dive deep into the mechanics behind the financial statements and zoom out to the industry trends.”

Sell | “At the time of writing, Moncler (OTCPK:MONRF) (OTCPK:MONRY) shares trade for €62. I believe that this price reflects overly optimistic future growth that does not align with its store expansions and revenue per store, not to mention the ongoing luxury fashion slowdown.”

-

Willow Tree Research | argenx: The Magic May Be Already Priced In

Biography: "Lifesciences investor focused on non-consensus long-short investment ideas."

Hold | “... argenx (ARGX) is a hold rating due to limited catalysts in 2025, rich valuation, and clinical risk around label expansion. I'll look elsewhere for value in a beaten-down biotech sector.”

-

Adrien Monnier | Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield: A Compromise Between Optimistic Growth And Lingering Issues

Biography: "My investing background includes corporate finance, M&A, and investment analysis, with a focus on real estate, renewable energy, and equity markets. I specialize in financial modelling, valuation, and qualitative analysis, demonstrated with hands-on roles in private equity, asset management and Real Estate."

Hold | "Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield's (OTCPK:UNBLF) 2024 earnings exceeded expectations, with a 40% dividend hike, reflecting confidence in cash flow stability and retail recovery. Despite operational successes, the Hamburg project cost overruns and execution risks raise concerns about future growth and management discipline."

-

Everyday Options | Meta Might Be Expensive On The Surface But It Has Long-Term Potential

Biography: "I’m an independent investor with a passion for exploring opportunities in options trading, analyzing dark pool activity, and understanding macroeconomic trends. With years of hands-on experience, I’ve developed a keen interest in identifying market trends and translating them into actionable ideas."

Buy | "Regulatory risks, competition, and economic factors pose challenges, but META's (META) strategic investments and favorable shifts in the competitive landscape provide reasons for cautious optimism."

-

The Alpha Analyst | U.S. Bancorp: A Contra Opportunity As Income Is Set To Rebound And Asset Quality Stabilizes

Biography: "I am a stock analyst with 20+ years of experience in quantitative research, financial modeling, and risk management. I specialize in equity valuation, market trends, and portfolio optimization to identify high-growth investment opportunities. My investment approach combines risk management with long-term value creation."

Buy | "I see U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) as a sound overlooked opportunity turning a corner, with stable fundamentals, low downside risks, and an attractive valuation compared to peers. The bank is showing signs of bottoming out in asset quality and overall improving fundamentals, positioning it well for a recovery — USB is likely to benefit greatly from any further rate cuts."

-

Mont Blanc Research | Duolingo's Language Exchange Expansion And Benefits From AI Advancements

Biography: "As a financial analyst, I specialize in B2C software and the internet content and information industries. I closely follow industry trends, opportunities, and challenges that companies in the software and internet industries face, analyzing how they will affect companies over the long term."

Buy | "The market is undervaluing Duolingo's (NASDAQ:DUOL) data moat and ability to integrate AI in its product that gives it long-term competitive advantage against AI chatbots. Declining AI costs will increase R&D efficiency, while a shift toward advanced courses will enhance user retention. Meanwhile, new products are expanding Duolingo’s reach into the language exchange space. These factors, along with Duolingo’s dominance in the rapidly growing online language learning industry, support a 'Buy' rating."

-

Pamplona Investments | Old But Gold: Hedging U.S. Equities Risk With Treasuries

Biography: "Inspired by Pamplona’s legendary bull run, we seek for tactical and long-term alpha opportunities through fundamental conviction coupled with strong quantitative insights. Unconstrained investment universe spanning across rates, credit, equities and derivatives strategies."

Buy | "If US equities were to enter a correction, international equities will almost certainly be negatively impacted. With that scenario becoming increasingly likely, I believe investors should start hedging their equities' exposure with an overweight in US treasuries at the long-end using SPDR Portfolio Long-Term Treasury ETF (SPTL)."

-

John F Coughlin | FIGS, Inc.: Slow Growth Is Still Growth

Biography: "A longtime investor with a general interest in healthcare equities. I take a holistic investment approach that considers top-down and bottom-up valuation techniques. I find the more minute details of companies, including their capital structure and debt covenants, play an outsized role in equity valuation, a point that I consider in all my evaluations of public companies."

Buy | "FIGS, Inc. (NYSE:FIGS), is an undervalued scrubs brand with substantial recurring revenue and a growing consumer base. Interest from the private equity firm Story3 Capital indicates the business's undervalued nature. While Q4 2024 earnings were less than ideal, the company's stock buyback program and strategic capital outlay present a price floor for investors."

-

Growth Stock Hunter | Tornado Infrastructure Equipment: A Growth Story

Biography: "My research approach integrates both fundamental and technical analysis, allowing me to identify strong growth stocks before they gain widespread attention. Key indicators I prioritize include relative strength, trading volume shifts, and accelerating profit growth—all of which help pinpoint stocks with the highest potential."

Buy | "Tornado Infrastructure Equipment (TGH:CA) is An attractive investment opportunity due to rapid revenue and profit growth, expanding product portfolio and increasing infrastructure market in the US. Although the US tariffs pose a risk, the management has drawn strategies to mitigate this impact, making the company an attractive growth investment."

-

Adam Davidson | ZIM Integrated Shipping: Long-Term Profitability Ahead

Biography: "I am a retail trader with over 10 years of experience managing my own personal investments including ETFs, Options, REITs, Stocks, Cryptocurrency, Commodities, Bonds, CDs, and Futures. I am interested in moderate- to high-risk investment growth via a mix of traditional and contrarian index investing, higher-risk, short-term plays, and generally exploring the best ways to make money within the market."

Buy | "The market is generally bearish on ZIM Integrated Shipping's (ZIM) future earnings prospects (due to bearish expectations for freight rates and given ZIM's 2023 losses) and this is reflected in the low market valuation. I believe the macro risks are overblown and ZIM is set to outperform the sector with a new, more efficient fleet and operational agility."

-

Ezequiel Szyrko | Fulgent Genetics: Undervalued, CFO Buying, And Promising Drug Candidates Make It A Buy

Biography: "I have nine years of experience in the markets as an independent investor and trader. My investing approaches are GARP, Value, and Growth. I closely follow insider buying and buybacks."

Buy | "Fulgent Genetics (FLGT) is a healthcare diagnostics & research company that is trading at 0.46x Price to Book Value. Right now, insiders own 39.4% of the total outstanding shares. The company has two promising drug candidates in its pipeline. Key trial results are expected at the ASCO meeting in May 2025."

-

LW Research | TransMedics: A Very Bullish Last Few Months Could Change Sentiment

Biography: "LW Research is run by a qualified accountant working in the financial services sector, mainly in private equity, hedge funds, asset management and venture capital. Since my time working as an accountant, I've become extremely well versed at diving into financials, analyzing risks, and seeing opportunities across a wide range of different companies."

Buy | "TransMedics' (TMDX) long-term growth potential is supported by its innovative organ transplant technology and ambitious goals for increasing transplant volumes. Despite macroeconomic uncertainties, TMDX appears undervalued compared to peers, making it a compelling buy under $80 for long-term growth."

-

Node Analytica Research | Bitcoin: Navigating A Correction Or Heading Into A Bear Market?

Biography: "Node Analytica is a technology and onchain research firm whose founders have developed systematic investment strategies in the digital assets market in order to generate competitive and uncorrelated investment returns for institutional investors."

Buy | "Bitcoin (BTC-USD) currently trades at ~$84,000, down 23% from its recent ATH of $109,000. On-chain analysis (NUPL, holder behavior) indicates the current correction resembles previous healthy pullbacks rather than signaling a prolonged bear market. Institutional adoption, notably Citadel Securities' entry, strengthens long-term bullish fundamentals."

-

Quality Contrarian Value | T. Rowe Price: Flow Stabilization Is The Turning Point

Biography: "I am currently working as an investment analyst at a family office. My specialty is identifying high-quality and/or severely mispriced investment ideas. I believe that a great investment idea would "jump" at you intuitively, and it comes in buying great companies at great prices."

Buy | "T. Rowe Price (TROW) is a misunderstood, undervalued asset manager offering a rare blend of high income and contrarian upside. While the market remains fixated on passive dominance and outflows, I believe the story is shifting. The key driver is flow stabilization — an inflection point that could unlock meaningful earnings growth and re-rate the stock's valuation."

-

Kennedy Njagi | Wells Fargo: A Classical Comeback Story Worth Playing!
